question: Which countries were elected as non-permanent members of the UN Security Council for the 2022-2023 term in June 2021?
ground_truth: Albania, Brazil, Gabon, Ghana, and the United Arab Emirates (UAE) were elected as non-permanent members.
event_desc: During the 2021 United Nations Security Council Elections, Brazil is elected to a two-year term as a non-permanent member of the UN Security Council, starting in 2022. It will mark the eleventh time Brazil has sat on the Security Council.
